Home.bigPictureImageAlt: Formation of autonomous systems at scale

bigPictureItems.text: Collaborative autonomy is becoming the difference between missions that finish and missions that stall. Contested environments push conventional control past its limits.
Operators already fly capable airframes. What they still need is multi-drone systems that run missions together, each with its own AI agent.
Spectr builds that sovereign autonomy layer in Norway for command, inspection, and logistics.

Home.roadmapTitle: Our capability and markets will continuously expand
Home.exclusionBanner: We maintain strict controls for export compliance, confidentiality, and sovereign deployment.
Home.launchTitle: We're launching the future of autonomy

autonomy: Expanded navigation resilience under GPS stress.

roadmapMilestones.summary: Operate at scale — collaborative autonomy across fleets totaling 100,000 drones in the field.

faq.question: What is collaborative multi-drone autonomy?
faq.answer: It is mission software that lets multiple drones run approved plans together — each with its own AI agent — so fleets keep executing when links degrade, GPS is denied, or operators cannot provide constant manual control.

faq.question: How is this different from a flight controller?
faq.answer: Flight controllers stabilize and fly the airframe. Spectr software sits above that stack and handles mission logic, collaboration between drones, and execution of approved plans — especially when the environment turns hostile.

faq.question: What environments can it operate in?
faq.answer: Spectr is built for contested and denied environments: jamming, GPS stress, and degraded datalinks. Missions stay scoped to approved objectives and constraints.

faq.question: Can missions be adjusted?
faq.answer: Yes. Operators can retask assets, update plans, intervene, or stop execution. Autonomy extends reach; it does not remove authority.

faq.question: Who benefits from this technology?
faq.answer: Defense and security operators who need collaborative fleets for inspection, logistics, ISR, and contested operations — with Norwegian ownership and national control over critical code.

faq.question: Is it safe and controllable?
faq.answer: Yes. Plans are operator-approved, scoped to mission constraints, and can be stopped instantly. Spectr designs for accountable autonomy, not uncontrolled behavior.

faq.question: Can operations be limited or avoided?
faq.answer: Yes. Deployment follows qualification, export controls, and customer rules of engagement. Spectr only supports approved use under national and allied compliance frameworks.

faq.question: What will operations look like on the ground?
faq.answer: Operators define goals, approve plans, and let Autonomous Engine execute across the fleet. Each drone runs its own AI agent while mission execution stays coherent under operator authority.

faq.question: How can I request this service?
faq.answer: Contact Spectr with your operational context and qualification needs. Start by trying Autonomous Engine, then request access for evaluation.

AutonomousEngine.title: Autonomous Engine
AutonomousEngine.tagline: Precise autonomy, exactly where the mission needs it.
AutonomousEngine.heroIntro: Imagine mission execution that is not limited by perfect links or constant manual control. Autonomous Engine keeps approved plans running when GPS is denied, datalinks degrade, or operators need fleets to continue without hand-flying every asset. Flight control stays on Betaflight — Spectr does not ship custom flight-controller firmware.
AutonomousEngine.overviewTitle: Built for the battlefield, not the lab
AutonomousEngine.overviewText: Autonomous Engine handles mission logic, navigation, and execution above the flight stack. Betaflight manages stabilization and manual flight modes on the airframe, while Spectr software coordinates approved plans when the environment turns hostile.

AutonomousEngine.flyDescription: Run approved mission plans through Autonomous Engine while Betaflight handles stabilization and manual flight control on the airframe.
AutonomousEngine.configureDescription: Tune rates, modes, and flight-controller settings in Betaflight. Spectr does not ship custom flight-controller firmware — configure the stack you already use, then connect Autonomous Engine at the mission layer.

Centurion.title: Centurion
Centurion.tagline: Sovereign mission command for mixed drone fleets.
Centurion.heroIntro: Imagine mission command that stays coherent when the environment turns hostile. Operators define goals and constraints; Centurion plans, coordinates, and reports across mixed fleets — with AI-assisted decision support built for real operations, not lab demos.

defense.title: Defense
defense.headline: Mission software for contested operations
defense.intro: Defense missions fail when links fail. Spectr keeps approved plans executing — and command workflows coherent — when conventional control models break down.
defense.body: From autonomous continuation on the airframe to mission command across fleets, Spectr software is built for operators who need capability that holds up in real contested environments.
benefits: Operator authority retained at every step
Integration with platforms already in the field

denied-environments.title: Denied Environments
denied-environments.headline: Operate when GPS and datalinks degrade
denied-environments.intro: Jamming and denial are the operating condition, not an edge case. Spectr software is designed so missions can continue when connectivity and positioning are under attack.
denied-environments.body: Autonomous Engine carries mission logic above the flight stack. Centurion keeps operators oriented as conditions change. Together they reduce dependence on perfect links.

isr.title: ISR
isr.headline: Sustain collection under pressure
isr.intro: ISR only matters if the asset can keep collecting and the operator can keep context. Spectr supports resilient execution and command for reconnaissance missions.
isr.body: Use Autonomous Engine to keep ISR profiles executing when links degrade, and Centurion to stage, coordinate, and report across collection assets.

fleet.title: Fleet Coordination
fleet.headline: Coordinate mixed fleets from one mission layer
fleet.intro: Operators fly diverse airframes. Spectr does not force a single platform — it adds a mission layer that plans and coordinates across the fleet you already have.
fleet.body: Centurion stages goals and constraints across assets. Autonomous Engine executes approved plans on the airframe when the environment demands autonomy.

offshore.title: Offshore
offshore.headline: Autonomy for offshore platforms and remote assets
offshore.intro: Offshore operations need fleets that keep inspecting and supporting assets when links degrade and conditions turn hostile.
offshore.body: Autonomous Engine keeps approved plans executing around platforms and remote sites, so operators cover more ground without constant manual control.
benefits: Platform and asset inspection under pressure

construction.title: Construction
construction.headline: Autonomous coverage for active construction sites
construction.intro: Construction sites change fast. Spectr helps fleets survey, monitor progress, and keep coverage consistent without constant manual flying.
construction.body: Use Autonomous Engine to run approved inspection and survey plans across the site so crews get clearer situational awareness as conditions shift.

field-ops.title: Field Operations
field-ops.headline: Extend reach for time-critical field work
field-ops.intro: Remote sites, short windows, and hostile environments demand software that works outside the lab. Spectr focuses on field readiness from day one.
field-ops.body: Whether the need is autonomous continuation, clearer command, or both, Spectr helps operators cover more ground with the fleets they already trust.

About.title: Our Team
About.heroIntro: Spectr builds collaborative multi-drone systems that autonomously run missions together — developed in Norway for real-world autonomous operations.
teamNarrative: Spectr exists because reliable autonomy is becoming the foundation of real-world operations. We are building collaborative multi-drone systems where each drone runs its own AI agent, coordinated through sovereign command and control software.

Our technology keeps missions running for inspection, logistics, ISR, and contested environments — when links degrade, GPS is denied, or operators need fleets to continue without constant manual control.

We do not manufacture drones. We build the mission layer above the stacks operators already fly, developed in Norway under national control. Humanity already flies capable airframes. What operators still need is software that lets those fleets work together.

Security.title: Security
Security.description: Spectr security, confidentiality, Norwegian ownership, and controlled infrastructure commitments.
Security.principlesHeadline: Verify sovereignty, from ownership to legal compliance.
Security.legalText1: Our operations are structured in accordance with the Norwegian Security Act (sikkerhetsloven) and the Business Security Regulation (virksomhetsikkerhetsforskriften § 83). We maintain organisational and technical safeguards for classified information and critical infrastructure under Norwegian law.
Security.legalText2: We believe critical technology should be developed, owned, and controlled locally — to safeguard Norwegian interests for the long term.
Security.nationalControl: We believe critical technology should be developed under full national control.

Legal.cookieIntro: This Cookie Policy explains how Spectr uses cookies and similar technologies on this website, and how you can manage your preferences.

Investor.headline: Sovereign autonomous drone software from Norway.
Investor.paragraph1: Spectr is developing Centurion — a sovereign mission command platform for multi-asset coordination, AI-assisted decision support, and autonomous operations across diverse drone fleets.
Investor.paragraph2: Investor relations inquiries are handled directly by the Spectr team. We share company materials, milestone updates, and strategic context with qualified stakeholders.
Investor.quote: Operators need mission software that keeps working when everything else fails.
